_readme: GENERATED by decision-engine/domain/parity_fixtures_test.go — DO NOT EDIT.
Regenerate: UPDATE_PARITY_FIXTURES=1 go test ./decision-engine/domain -run TestEngineParityFixtures
Every expectation below is computed by the REAL engine (domain.Execute). The vitest
web/src/lib/demo/engine-parity.test.ts replays each case through the demo walker
(web/src/lib/demo/walk.ts) and asserts behavioral parity: status, final output,
trace node-id sequence, per-node outputs, reason codes, failed node, and (for
failures) that the demo error CONTAINS error_contains (the Go error with the
expr-lang position/snippet suffix stripped).
Numbers are compared after a JSON round-trip, so Go int/float distinctions that
JSON erases (int 1 vs float64 1) are equal by design; float64 arithmetic is
bit-identical between Go and JS, so values like 0.30000000000000004 match exactly.
DOCUMENTED EXCLUSIONS (not fixtured):
1. code nodes: Go runs Starlark; the demo runs the shared assignment subset
(`name = expr` lines, data['field'] reads, # comments). Control flow, def,
//, **, negative modulo (Python floors, JS truncates) and Starlark's own
error texts (beyond the empty-code guard) are outside the shared subset.
2. predict/connect/ai: fixtures inject PRE-RESOLVED buckets (the Go shell's
seam) which both engines echo identically. Missing-provider failures are
not fixtured — the demo always has its in-browser providers.
3. expr-lang features beyond the demo grammar (builtins, `in`, ranges, string
repeat, closures) are not fixtured.
4. runtime-format type errors on deep-path values (e.g. `nested.b + 1` →
'invalid operation: <nil> + int') are not fixtured; the demo reports
checker-format messages for those. Deep-path reads, equality and ?? ARE
fixtured (they succeed identically).
5. demo-authoring dialect config forms (split edges carrying condition
expressions, output-node assignments, manual_review literal configs, code
`source` DSL) are demo sugar the real engine rejects; the parity surface is
the real engine's config forms, which the demo executes with Go semantics.
6. suspended-run resume is not fixtured (the demo resumes through its store
shell); the suspend state itself IS fixtured (record + case fields).
